heading: Art. 121. Larceny and wrongful appropriation
label: 921 Art. 121. Larceny and wrongful appropriation
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)921(a)921(b)50:715(a).50:715(b).(Art. 121), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 140In subsection (a), the words “whatever” and “true” are omitted as surplusage. The word “it” is substituted for the words “the same” in clauses (1) and (2).
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)921(a)921(b)50:715(a).50:715(b).(Art. 121), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 140In subsection (a), the words “whatever” and “true” are omitted as surplusage. The word “it” is substituted for the words “the same” in clauses (1) and (2).

children.text: with intent permanently to deprive or defraud another person of the use and benefit of property or to appropriate it to his own use or the use of any person other than the owner, steals that property and is guilty of larceny; or

with intent temporarily to deprive or defraud another person of the use and benefit of property or to appropriate it to his own use or the use of any person other than the owner, is guilty of wrongful appropriation.

children.label: b
children.text: Any person found guilty of larceny or wrongful appropriation shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
